meaning: adj.代用的,合成的,假的,人造的
examples: The ersatz spontaneity of "Sunday Love" sounds especially hollow.
As ersatz investment Banks, they make short - and long-term loans and take equity stakes.
And this solemn, ersatz epic not only misses the boat intellectually but also fails as entertainment.
They and the Italians are enduring an ersatz recovery, derivative of more authentic revivals elsewhere.
Keating learned about the scam only when he saw one of his ersatz Bennetts in the gallery windows.
